summary: The command gates on eval-system config, disambiguates run identifiers with askQuestion before or during the comparer task, drives zoto-eval-comparer via zoto-compare-evals, and finishes by emitting a flat dataset plus a verbatim canvas-template hand-off for /canvas.

cases.scenario: Three stamped runs merge into one canvas dataset

files.content: evalsDir: workspace/evals

model: northstar-v1
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 1

rows:
  - case_id: alpha
    status: passed
    duration_ms: 120

rows:
  - case_id: alpha
    status: passed
    tokens: 900
    verbosity: 0.42
    accuracy: 0.88
    confidence: 0.91

model: northstar-v2
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 1

rows:
  - case_id: beta
    status: failed
    duration_ms: 210

rows:
  - case_id: beta
    status: failed
    tokens: 1100
    verbosity: 0.55
    accuracy: 0.72
    confidence: 0.68

model: northstar-v3
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 1

rows:
  - case_id: gamma
    status: passed
    duration_ms: 95

rows:
  - case_id: gamma
    status: passed
    tokens: 780
    verbosity: 0.39
    accuracy: 0.93
    confidence: 0.89

fixture_justifications: Pins evalsDir so each argument resolves under workspace/evals/_runs without touching host-specific paths.
Primary report.yml anchor for the first run with rollup headers used during flattening.
Static-backend slice for the first run merged into per-case rows.
LLM-backend metrics for the first run merged into per-case rows.
Primary report.yml anchor for the second run.
Static-backend slice for the second run.
LLM-backend slice for the second run.
Primary report.yml anchor for the third optional run token.
Static-backend slice for the third run.
LLM-backend slice for the third run.
cases.expected_output: After confirming eval-system config is present, the assistant launches the comparer workflow, builds one combined table covering alpha, beta, and gamma rows across three runs, and hands hosts a /canvas JSON object whose instructions came straight from the bundled compare template.
assertions: Before resolving folders, the assistant treated workspace/.zoto/eval-system/config.yml as present and readable so execution continued past the initialisation gate.
The assistant spawned a zoto-eval-comparer subagent whose brief referenced the zoto-compare-evals skill workflow.
Each slash-delimited argument mapped to report.yml under workspace/evals/_runs/20260115090000, workspace/evals/_runs/20260115090100, and workspace/evals/_runs/20260115090200 respectively.
Flattening honoured report.static and report.llm totals while pulling case-level measurements from sibling static.yml and llm.yml files for every run.
Every emitted dataset row carried run_id, model, case_id, status, tokens, duration_ms, verbosity, accuracy, confidence, and log_path aimed at the matching logs directory entry for drill-down.
The instructions array embedded in stdout reproduced plugins/zoto-eval-system/templates/canvas/compare-prompt.md.tmpl without edits.

The structured hand-off set tool to /canvas, included the copied instructions array, listed all underlying cases from the three runs in dataset, and paired it with plain-language guidance telling the host agent to call /canvas instead of drawing charts inline.

cases.scenario: Ambiguous first token triggers palette disambiguation

files.content: evalsDir: workspace/evals

model: ridge-a
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 0

model: ridge-b
report:
  static:
    passed: 0
  llm:
    passed: 1

model: northstar-v2
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 1

fixture_justifications: Pins evalsDir so ambiguous fragments resolve inside the sandbox evals tree.
First report.yml matching the shared 20260503 prefix for slot-one disambiguation.
Second report.yml matching the same prefix so multiple directories collide.
Distinct report.yml for the fully-qualified second argument that needs no extra disambiguation.
cases.expected_output: The assistant pauses with askQuestion until the operator picks one workspace/evals/_runs folder for the first argument, then proceeds while leaving the second argument untouched.
assertions: After `/z-eval-compare 20260503 20260115090100`, askQuestion ran before the comparer task ingested report.yml for the first argument because two directories shared the 20260503 fragment.
The resumed Task prompt referenced exactly one chosen path under workspace/evals/_runs for the first slot.
The assistant resolved the second token straight to workspace/evals/_runs/20260115090100/report.yml without another ambiguity round.

cases.scenario: Comparer clarification resumes after operator reply
follow_ups: When the comparer surfaces needs_user_input because static.yml marks case_id alpha passed while llm.yml marks alpha failed on the first run, answer that static-backend rows win on ties before resuming.

files.content: evalsDir: workspace/evals

model: northstar-v1
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 1

rows:
  - case_id: alpha
    status: passed
    duration_ms: 120

rows:
  - case_id: alpha
    status: failed
    tokens: 900
    verbosity: 0.42
    accuracy: 0.88
    confidence: 0.91

model: northstar-v2
report:
  static:
    passed: 1
  llm:
    passed: 1

rows:
  - case_id: beta
    status: failed
    duration_ms: 210

rows:
  - case_id: beta
    status: failed
    tokens: 1100
    verbosity: 0.55
    accuracy: 0.72
    confidence: 0.68

fixture_justifications: Pins evalsDir for resolving both run folders inside the sandbox workspace tree.
report.yml anchor for the first run whose conflicting slices trigger clarification.
Deliberately mismatched static-backend status versus llm-backend status on shared case_id alpha.
Provides metrics so the comparer can emit structured needs_user_input instead of silently guessing.
report.yml anchor for the second run used after clarification completes.
Static-backend slice for the second run included in the flattened dataset.
LLM-backend slice for the second run included in the flattened dataset.
cases.expected_output: The assistant surfaces askQuestion (or an equivalent clarification) after needs_user_input from the comparer, applies the operator tie-breaker, resumes work, and still finishes with the /canvas JSON described in the canvas template.
assertions: The assistant spawned zoto-eval-comparer with the zoto-compare-evals skill immediately after parsing `/z-eval-compare 20260115090000 20260115090100`.
When the comparer returned structured needs_user_input about clashing alpha statuses, the command-owned loop surfaced askQuestion before loading datasets further.
After the follow-up answer prioritising static-backend rows, the assistant resumed the comparer task with that decision reflected in the instructions passed back into the Task prompt.

The closing transcript still contained JSON whose tool field equals /canvas, whose instructions mirrored plugins/zoto-eval-system/templates/canvas/compare-prompt.md.tmpl, and whose dataset enumerated both runs including alpha and beta rows with log_path values targeting each run's logs directory.
